user_query:
Our nightly backup validation for survivor stories is failing, and I need an actionable, verifiable incident snapshot plus a runbook update. Please do the following using only the provided files and your local environment:

1) Run the validator and analyze its command output and error messages:
   - Command to run: python3 scripts/validate_backup.py
   - Capture the process exit code and parse the output. Treat any line beginning with "ERROR" as an error record with fields file_path, type, and detail (as shown in the line). Use the SUMMARY/INFO lines only to cross-check counts.

2) Create a structured incident report at out/incident_report.json with this exact top-level structure:
   - incident_id: "backup-validation-sample-001"
   - input_dir: value reported in the validator INFO line
   - validator_exit_code: integer exit code from running the validator
   - totals: an object with scanned, ok, and errors all as integers, matching the validator’s SUMMARY/INFO
   - errors: an array of objects derived strictly from the ERROR lines; each object must have: file_path (relative path emitted by the script), error_type (value from type=...), and detail (value from detail=...)
   - recommendations: at least 3 concrete actions tailored to these findings (e.g., enforce required fields at intake, exclude non-JSON files, add a pre-commit JSON schema check). Do not invent errors that aren’t present in the output.

3) Also write a CSV at out/affected_files.csv with header: file_path,error_type. Include one row per error in the same order as in out/incident_report.json.

4) Edit docs/runbook.md to reflect this incident by replacing the single line that currently says "TODO: fill after incident" with a new subsection titled exactly:
   Incident: Backup validation failures (sample-001)
   The subsection must contain three short labeled parts: Summary (one paragraph describing what failed and why, grounded in the errors you found), Impact (who/what is affected and to what extent, referencing file counts and filenames as appropriate), and Mitigations (a concise list of steps we will take, aligned with your recommendations in the JSON report). Keep the rest of the document unchanged.

Constraints and checks:
- Do not modify any inputs except docs/runbook.md.
- The totals in out/incident_report.json must match the validator's SUMMARY/INFO lines for the run you perform.
- Every error listed in the JSON and CSV must correspond to an ERROR line from the validator output.
- Use the configuration at config/backup_config.json and the input directory data/stories as provided.

Deliverables to create/update:
- out/incident_report.json
- out/affected_files.csv
- docs/runbook.md (with the TODO line replaced as specified)

This is for our disaster relief advocacy efforts; I need a reliable snapshot I can share with our team before we publish anonymized stories. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
